name: aligned_zero_size
reason: If aligned(addr, 0) is always true, any address is vacuously "aligned" to size zero, which is meaningless and could mask alignment-check bugs.

name: inv_allows_empty_arch
code:
proof fn phi_2_inv_allows_empty_arch(a: Arch)
    requires
        a.layers.len() == 0,
    ensures
        a.inv(),
{
}
reason: If an architecture with zero layers satisfies inv(), the invariant is too weak—an empty page table hierarchy is nonsensical and would let downstream proofs vacuously hold.

name: upper_vaddr_equals_base
code:
proof fn phi_3_upper_vaddr_equals_base(a: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        a.inv(),
        layer < a.layers.len(),
    ensures
        a.upper_vaddr(layer, base) == base,
{
}
reason: If upper_vaddr equals the base, the mapped address range has zero size, meaning the layer maps nothing—defeating the purpose of the page table level.

name: entry_base_ignores_base
code:
proof fn phi_4_entry_base_ignores_base(a: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        a.inv(),
        layer < a.layers.len(),
        base > 0,
    ensures
        a.entry_base(layer, base, 0) != base,
{
}
reason: entry_base at index 0 must equal the base address; if it doesn't, the arithmetic is broken and all derived virtual addresses would be shifted incorrectly.

name: child_layer_entry_not_smaller
code:
proof fn phi_5_child_layer_entry_not_smaller(a: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        a.inv(),
        i + 1 < a.layers.len(),
    ensures
        a.entry_size((i + 1) as nat) >= a.entry_size(i),
{
}
reason: In a well-formed page table hierarchy, inner layers must map strictly smaller regions than outer layers; if a child layer's entry_size is >= the parent's, the hierarchical decomposition is violated.
